_schema.status: live | coming-soon. Live entries must have file.tag/name/sha256; the site build downloads the asset, verifies the sha256, and extracts name/description/instructions from the .adf itself. Coming-soon entries render from the curated fields here (no file yet).
_schema.naming: Agents get botanical given names (ash, sage, hazel, fern, ...). The family signature is the filename form: lowercase name + .adf. Job/role lives in tagline and category, never in the name.
_schema.capabilities: Curated disclosure of what the agent uses: channels, tools, mcp, sandbox (js|container|host), network. Shown on the agent page so people know what they're opening.

agents.tagline: A portable personal assistant that learns your environment and improves through use.

agents.name: sage
agents.tagline: An ADF expert. Ask it anything about the format, the spec, or ADF Studio.
agents.description: Sage knows the ADF specification, ADF Studio, and the runtime inside out. Keep it beside you while you build: it answers questions about the format, debugs agent configs, and explains what every table in a .adf does.

agents.name: willow
agents.tagline: An agent that builds agents. Describe what you need and it raises a new .adf.
agents.description: Willow scaffolds new agents: give it a goal and it creates the .adf, writes the instructions, wires the tools and channels, and hands you a file ready to grow.
